subject: LU-65 no error when close if application has known write failure

messages.message:
Patch Set 1: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/425/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1: No score

Build Started http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/432/

Patch Set 1: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/432/ : ABORTED

Patch Set 1: No score

Build Started http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/438/

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Fanyong, could you please clarify why we need this patch and how it is related to 1.8/2.1 interop?
The problem with this patch is that errors on async i/o won't be returned on fsync any more if a sync write failed at some point, e.g.:

sync_write(fd)   -> EIO
sync_rewrite(fd) -> success
async_write(fd)  -> success
(async writes failed in the background)
fsync            -> success

lli_write_rc (unlike lli_async_rc) is never cleared.

Patch Set 1:

Johann, sorry I do not understand your question. The purpose of the this patch is to prevent error reporting repeatedly, which is not related with 1.8<->2.x interoperability. For example, if some async write failed, then it is unnecessary to report error when close, which will trigger ll_flush(). On the other hand, if sync/async write succeed, then "lli_write_rc" will be cleared.

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/516/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

> Johann, sorry I do not understand your question.
> The purpose of the this patch is to prevent error
> reporting repeatedly, which is not related with
> 1.8<->2.x interoperability.

You submitted this patch under LU-65 which is "Interop testing results for 1.8.5.54 clients with Lustre 2.0.59 servers". This bug is quite overloaded.

> For example, if some async write failed, then it is
> unnecessary to report error when close, which will
> trigger ll_flush(). On the other hand, if sync/async
> write succeed, then "lli_write_rc" will be cleared.

Again, i don't think this is correct. If a write fails with ENOSPC or EAGAIN, we should still report errors on close if some previous async writes failed.

Patch Set 2:

>> For example, if some async write failed, then it is > unnecessary to report error when close, which will > trigger ll_flush(). On the other hand, if sync/async > write succeed, then "lli_write_rc" will be cleared.
>Again, i don't think this is correct. If a write fails with ENOSPC or EAGAIN, we should still report errors on close if some previous async writes failed.

If some write fails with ENOSPC/EAGAIN/EDQUOT, or something else, which maybe caused by former async write operations, then application usually closes related file and exit. At such time, since the application has known write failure, why the close operation return failure again?

Patch Set 2:

> if some write fails with ENOSPC/EAGAIN/EDQUOT, or something
> else, which maybe caused by former async write operations, > then application usually closes related file and exit.
> At such time, since the application has known write
> failure, why the close operation return failure again?

The application might not consider some of those errors as fatal or could think that only the last write was unsuccessful. Therefore i think we should still notify the application of other errors on async writes (like EIO) that might have happened.
Also, EINTR can be returned on write(2) and should not mask previous errors.

Patch Set 2:

>The application might not consider some of those errors as fatal or could think that only the last write was unsuccessful. Therefore i think we should still notify the application of other errors on async writes (like EIO) that might have happened. Also, EINTR can be returned on write(2) and should not mask previous errors.

But It is not all applications will ignore close error (returned by ll_flush), for some applications, you maybe get confused error. For example:
===============
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [dd] [if=/dev/zero] [of=/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1] [bs=1024] [count=9410] [seek=9410]
dd: writing `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1': Disk quota exceeded
dd: closing output file `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1': Input/output error
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [dd] [if=/dev/zero] [of=/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1] [bs=1024] [count=1024] [seek=18821]
dd: writing `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1': Disk quota exceeded
dd: closing output file `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-1': Input/output error
0
===============

What we expected is something like this:
===============
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [dd] [if=/dev/zero] [of=/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-0] [bs=1024] [count=14631] [seek=14631]
dd: writing `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-0': Disk quota exceeded
13182+0 records in
13181+0 records out
13497344 bytes (13 MB) copied, 0.999215 seconds, 13.5 MB/s
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [dd] [if=/dev/zero] [of=/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-0] [bs=1024] [count=1024] [seek=29262]
dd: writing `/mnt/lustre/d0.sanity-quota/d1/f1-0': Disk quota exceeded
1+0 records in
0+0 records out
0 bytes (0 B) copied, 0.00409484 seconds, 0.0 kB/s
0
===============

Patch Set 2:

In fact, if the write failure is returned by "ll_file_aio_write()", then it is out of the control of "lli_async_rc". Maybe we can use more complex logic to trace all write related failures by single "lli_async_rc", but the separate "ll_write_rc" is quite simple to resolve these issues.

I have opened a new bug "LU-359" to trace this issue and more discussion if necessary.

Patch Set 2: Abandoned

Replaced by http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,596

commit.subject: LU-65 no error when close if application has known write failure
commit.message:
LU-65 no error when close if application has known write failure

Do not return error when close if application has known write failure already.

Change-Id: I4f23d196e7a3563741477942d9130aa35e1d396a
Signed-off-by: nasf <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
